dark mode on tv
i've been using spotify since 2022 now and every night to sleep i play music on my tv in my room. dark mode has never been an issue, i've loved it all the years i've used it since it prevents light from being in my face when i'm trying to sleep. so imagine my surprise when spotify makes the grand decision to take it off like they have good sense or something. on top of that, when i try to listen to music on my TV now it eventually just turns off the app when it's never done that before so i also suspect that's spotify's fault. dark mode wasn't harming anything and there was literally zero good reason to remove it. 100% ruined half of my experience :/

Emotional support 101
I haven’t written one of these reviews in so long. When I last wrote a review it was about how excited I was about a skip ad feature, I got premium and it changes a whole perspective in Spotify for sure, for free it’s also “free” and adaptable. The music quality is so good I almost cry to every song. There is so much more than music like podcasts and even those have the best quality. This app has been there through my highs and lows too, when I fell in love, and when I felt alone. No matter what Spotify was the music app that always came to my heart and I always felt a bond, not one where I could speak face to face but something that would listen to me just by me listening to the songs set out. Songs set out and recommended are very personalized, sure some are those “random song I dislike” however after long enough it turns into more “songs I really love” there is so many specific playlists made specifically for the user, I love that there is so many of them. I suggest someone use Spotify at least ONCE in their life, it’s really worth it. Thank you Spotify for being in my life

Advertisement Bombardment
Listening to music on this app is simply the highlight of my day, listening to the same Metallica songs constantly gives me an indescribable and surreal feeling of immense bliss. BUT, like all good things, it had to come to an end. For context, there is a 1:30-2:00 minute advertisement break per 10 minutes of listening. Anyways, the basis of my review is that the seemingly constant stream of stupid advertisements from the same four companies becomes redundant and ruins the overall listening experience. All though Spotify’s music and podcast catalog is top-tier, the advertisement bombardment is the one shortcoming that doesn’t put my review at five stars. Thank you for reading my rant about advertising. :3

Just a great consistent app
Despite all the political pressure of removing ourselves from corruption; I stood by Spotify. They’re the best music app that exists today. Having no ads, doesn’t break the bank; or violate my choice to pay for no ads to then be r worded or “lolested”with. Frankly, my personal political views won’t interfere with a corporation that consistently gives to their users. Their prices are never outrageous; they’re always finding new ways to incorporate new music to our playlist and then other companies follow *their* algorithm and gain all the credit because of moralistic lmnop. I love you Spotify since 2012 USA ❤️👏

Too many repeats
I’ve used Spotify for over a decade and I’ve always loved it, until recently. The shuffle algorithm has been playing the same handful of songs, over and over again. I have playlists with over 24hrs worth of music, and Spotify will play the same 20 songs over and over. Also, as of late, I will skip a song just to hear it again not 6 skips later. Disappointing and frustrating.

Overall View of Spotify
Don’t get me wrong, I did enjoy trying other music apps, but Spotify is probably my favorite. I love how easy it is to find my favorite indie bands, musical songs, and songs from shows I adore. Honestly, I’m also a sucker for how easy it is to customize my playlists because I like knowing which playlist has what music on it based off of the vibe of its album cover. On the contrary, unfortunately there things I dislike, one of them being that I cannot download my playlists unless I pay for premium, which is something I REFUSE to do because it is honestly stupid that I should have to pay for something as simple as that. I also don’t like that I can’t take my music off of shuffle unless I have premium. It’s weird to listen to musical music out of order. The adds are understandable because Spotify needs to make money somehow, but I could definitely that becoming/staying as a common complaint.

Best thing on my phone
Spotify is my favorite app and probably the thing I use the most every day. I listen to music when I’m at school, at home, working out, or just relaxing. It has almost every song I want to hear, and it’s really easy to make playlists and discover new music. The recommendations are always good, and I like how it learns my music taste over time. Music helps me focus, feel better when I’m stressed, and have fun, and Spotify makes that possible. I honestly use Spotify more than any other app, and I can’t imagine going a day without it. I definitely recommend it to anyone who loves music.

Bug and subscription issues
The app is one of the best music apps there is. The ability to make a ton of different playlists is excellent. My only two issues are that Spotify stops working in my car when I play any audiobook. My next issue is that there is a finite amount of audiobook listening time on the app. As someone who drives a lot, I would much rather pay a higher price for unlimited audiobook downloads and listens. I think an extra $11 a month for an extra 30 hours is ridiculous.

A cheat
What I am about to say is probably pointless since most people have had a good experience and this app has a good raising, but trust me when I say it doesn’t need a good review. At first I thought that this app was the best thing ever for listening to music and podcasts. But, the longer I have had the app the worse it has gotten. For one, I can only change the song six times an hour! This makes it highly annoying and I keep having to exit off the app and completely change the thread and it is highly annoying. Then, sometimes it won’t even let me listen to the song I choose. Honestly, I think listening to music on YouTube is better than this stuff. And when I do decide to listen to music I only get to listen to a few songs before having to go through 5 minutes worth of adds, which I don’t have the patience for. And what’s even more stupid is that to fix all these issues, I have to pay for Spotify premium!!! Like, I am sorry, but I don’t have the money and the time to deal with this!! Don’t get this app 😡😡😡

Enjoy this- but do something about it

My Escape
This app has become my go‑to sanctuary. Building playlists isn’t just fun — it’s grounding. In the middle of a chaotic life, it gives me a place to slow down, feel free, and reconnect with myself.

Am now a believer in lossless.
A friend of mine at work forwarded a free month special to try this out. I have probably around 15-20k$ on n my truck audio system and appreciate sound quality.  I miss the ol days of CD quality but this is seriously on par with SQ.   I find myself listening to many of my old regular songs because I hear things I havnt heard in years. This has been a breath of fresh air and do not mind adding it to my monthly streaming expenses.  Well worth it.
